_readme: Source of truth for /lfg/[id] guide pages. Each entry describes how to find groups for that game on Weered. Voice rules: honest about native game LFG, specific to the game's culture, useful as a real guide not SEO filler.

guides.tldr: Open the Destiny 2 lobby on Weered, pick a room matched to the activity (raid, dungeon, GM, Trials, Pantheon), post your fireteam slot with your Bungie ID, power level, and what you're running. Weered links your Bungie account once and your activity results flow back through the PGCR — so verified clears, tournament credit, and stat tracking happen automatically.

native_lfg.name: Bungie Fireteam Finder
native_lfg.honest_take: Bungie's in-game Fireteam Finder (launched 2024) is solid for one-off pickup activities. It's quick, lives inside the Director, and works. Where it falls short is everything *around* the run: persistent crews that survive past the activity, tournaments verified through the API, sherpa relationships that build over weeks, and post-run discussion. Weered's Destiny 2 lobby covers that wraparound layer.

activity_types.name: Raids
activity_types.examples: Salvation's Edge, Crota's End, Last Wish, Vow of the Disciple
activity_types.typical_filters: Power level, weapon loadout familiarity, sherpa-friendly or experienced

activity_types.name: Dungeons
activity_types.examples: Warlord's Ruin, Ghosts of the Deep, Spire of the Watcher
activity_types.typical_filters: Power level, solo/duo flawless attempts, casual clear

activity_types.name: Grandmaster Nightfalls
activity_types.typical_filters: Power level cap, champion mods loaded, surge weapons

activity_types.name: Trials of Osiris
activity_types.typical_filters: Glory rank, mic required, three-peeking, 5+ win card

activity_types.name: Pantheon 2.0
activity_types.typical_filters: Encounter familiarity, score pushing, casual completion

activity_types.name: Crucible
activity_types.typical_filters: Rank, mic, comp seriousness

activity_types.name: Custom Ops
activity_types.typical_filters: Modifier preference, score target

guides.weered_advantage: Activity completions are verified server-side via the Bungie Post-Game Carnage Report (PGCR). Your tournament entries, challenge progress, and stat history are tied to actual API data — no trust-based 'I cleared it, I promise.' That same integration powers persistent crew membership and verified-tournament hosting that Bungie's own tools don't offer.

faq.q: Do I need to link my Bungie account to find a fireteam on Weered?
faq.a: You can join the lobby and post LFG without linking. Bungie account linking is required only if you want your activity results to count toward tournaments, challenges, or persistent crew stats. Most casual fireteam-finding doesn't need it.

faq.q: Is Weered's fireteam finder different from Bungie's in-game Fireteam Finder?
faq.a: Yes. Bungie's tool is great for instant pickup fireteams within the Director. Weered focuses on what happens around the activity: persistent crews that stay together past one run, verified-via-PGCR tournament results, sherpa relationships, and post-run discussion in the forum.

faq.q: Can I find a fireteam for Pantheon 2.0 or GM Nightfalls on Weered?
faq.a: Yes. The Destiny 2 lobby on Weered has pinned rooms for raids, dungeons, GMs, Trials, Pantheon 2.0 races, and Custom Ops challenges. Each room is built for that specific activity's group-finding needs.

faq.q: Is it free to find a fireteam on Weered?
faq.a: Yes. Joining the Destiny 2 lobby and using the LFG system is free. Premium features (custom community branding, cosmetic flair) are optional paid extras.

guides.tldr: Open the League of Legends lobby on Weered, pick a room matched to your queue (Solo/Duo, Flex, Premier, Clash, ARAM), post your role preference, current rank, and what you're queueing for. Weered's Riot API integration shows your verified summoner profile, ranked tier, and top champion mastery so prospective teammates can size up fit before queueing.

native_lfg.name: Riot Clash and in-client looking-for-team
native_lfg.honest_take: Riot's in-client team finder handles ad-hoc queue partners well, and Clash bracket signups handle the tournament side. Both are oriented around immediate queue fill rather than persistent community. Weered fills the gap for finding the same crew week after week, ranking up together, and coordinating Clash teams that actually communicate before tournament day.

activity_types.name: Ranked Solo/Duo
activity_types.typical_filters: Rank, role main, peak rank, time zone

activity_types.name: Flex Queue 5-stack
activity_types.typical_filters: Rank, role coverage, mic required

activity_types.name: Premier Division Teams
activity_types.typical_filters: Division tier, role mains, scrim time

activity_types.name: Clash Tournament Teams
activity_types.typical_filters: Tier eligibility, role coverage, full availability for one bracket window

activity_types.name: ARAM stacks
activity_types.typical_filters: Casual vibe, no rank pressure

activity_types.name: Coaching / VOD review
activity_types.typical_filters: Rank gap, specific role focus, free or paid

guides.weered_advantage: Weered pulls your live Riot API data — verified summoner name, current ranked tier, top champion mastery, recent match history — so when you post LFG, prospective teammates can see who you actually are without having to trust a screenshot. The challenger leaderboard and free champion rotation live in the lobby too.

faq.q: Do I need to link my Riot account to find a team on Weered?
faq.a: You can post LFG without linking, but linking your Riot ID lets the lobby pull your verified rank, role mains, and recent match data. That makes your LFG posts more credible and helps teammates filter for fit.

faq.q: Can I find Clash teammates on Weered?
faq.a: Yes. The League lobby has a Clash room for assembling and coordinating tournament rosters ahead of weekend brackets. Posting includes your tier eligibility, role coverage, and full-bracket availability.

faq.q: Does Weered show ranked stats for League players?
faq.a: Yes — verified through the Riot API. Summoner profile, current Solo/Duo and Flex tier, top champion mastery, and recent matches all pull live from Riot.

faq.q: Is the League lobby on Weered free?
faq.a: Yes. Joining and finding teammates is free. Premium tier exists for cosmetic flair and custom community branding but doesn't gate any LFG functionality.

guides.tldr: Open the MTG lobby on Weered, post your format (Commander, Modern, cEDH, brew night, sealed), power level (1-10 for Commander, deck archetype for other formats), and platform (paper webcam, MTGO, Spelltable, Cockatrice, Tabletop Simulator). Weered's chat embeds Scryfall card lookups via [[Card Name]] syntax and renders Moxfield/Archidekt deck links as enriched chips with commander, colors, and card count.

native_lfg.name: Discord Commander pod servers + Spelltable LFG
native_lfg.honest_take: The MTG LFG landscape is fragmented across Discord servers (per-format, per-region), the official Spelltable lobby, and various Facebook groups. None of them have rich Scryfall + deck-platform integration in chat. Weered's MTG lobby leans into that integration: paste a Moxfield link, see the deck details inline; type [[Sol Ring]], see the card image. Makes pod-finding conversations way faster.

activity_types.name: Commander / EDH pods
activity_types.typical_filters: Power level 1-10, salt level, proxy policy, time zone

activity_types.name: cEDH (competitive Commander)
activity_types.typical_filters: Deck tier, no proxy / proxy-friendly, fast play

activity_types.name: Brew night
activity_types.examples: Jank decks, weird strategies, no-tier-list zone
activity_types.typical_filters: Anti-meta vibe, casual feedback

activity_types.name: Modern / Pioneer / Legacy
activity_types.typical_filters: Format, archetype, platform (MTGO/paper)

activity_types.name: Limited (sealed, draft)
activity_types.typical_filters: Cube list, set, schedule

activity_types.name: Cube draft
activity_types.typical_filters: Cube link, schedule, platform

guides.weered_advantage: Inline Scryfall card lookups ([[Card Name]] in chat), Moxfield + Archidekt deck-link embeds with commander, color identity, and card count rendered automatically, and persistent rooms so the same Friday-night pod doesn't have to re-find each other every week. Lobby presence shows who's online and what platform (paper, MTGO, Spelltable).

faq.q: How do I find a Commander pod on Weered?
faq.a: Open the MTG lobby, go to the Commander room, post your power level (1-10), play schedule, and platform (paper webcam, Spelltable, MTGO, Tabletop Simulator). Other players reply with their decks (Moxfield/Archidekt links render with commander, colors, and card count inline).

faq.q: Does Weered have card image previews in chat?
faq.a: Yes. Type [[Card Name]] in any MTG-lobby chat and the card image, mana cost, and oracle text render inline via Scryfall. Works for any printed card.

faq.q: Can I share Moxfield or Archidekt decks in the MTG lobby?
faq.a: Yes — paste the deck URL and it renders as a rich chip showing commander, color identity, card count, and format. No need to open a separate tab to evaluate someone's deck.

faq.q: Does Weered support cEDH or just casual Commander?
faq.a: Both. The Commander room covers casual pods (power 1-7), and the cEDH room handles optimized 4-player cEDH with no-proxy/proxy-friendly filtering.

guides.tldr: Open the Helldivers 2 lobby on Weered, pick a room matched to your difficulty (Trivial through Super Helldive), post the planet/sector you're spreading democracy to, your loadout preference, and mic requirement. Weered's lobby tracks the live war map so squads coordinate around current Major Orders and faction pushes.

native_lfg.name: In-game SOS beacon + Steam friends
native_lfg.honest_take: Helldivers 2's SOS beacon does its job for ad-hoc joiners on a mission in progress. Where it falls short is everything pre-deployment: planning a Helldive run on a specific planet, coordinating Major Order pushes, building a recurring squad that drops together every week. The Weered Helldivers lobby fills that planning + persistent-squad layer.

activity_types.name: Helldive / Super Helldive runs
activity_types.typical_filters: Difficulty cap, mic required, faction (Terminid/Automaton/Illuminate), loadout coordination

activity_types.name: Major Order pushes
activity_types.typical_filters: Specific planet/sector, sample farming vs liberation focus

activity_types.name: Sample / super-credit farming
activity_types.typical_filters: Difficulty, planet, mission type

activity_types.name: Casual democracy
activity_types.typical_filters: Chill vibe, mic optional, any difficulty

guides.weered_advantage: Lobby includes a live war map module showing current Major Orders, planet status, and faction pressure — squads form around the actual community objectives, not abstract LFG. Persistent rooms mean the same squad you ran with on Wednesday isn't lost on Friday.

faq.q: Can I find a Helldive squad on Weered?
faq.a: Yes. The Helldivers 2 lobby has rooms for Helldive and Super Helldive difficulty, plus rooms for Major Order pushes and lower-difficulty casual squads. Post your loadout, faction preference, and mic requirement.

faq.q: Does Weered show the live Helldivers 2 war map?
faq.a: Yes. The lobby has a war map module pulling live community-objective data so squads form around current Major Orders and active planet pushes.

faq.q: Is the Helldivers 2 lobby on Weered free?
faq.a: Yes — joining and finding squads is free. Premium tier exists for cosmetic flair but doesn't gate any LFG functionality.

faq.q: Do I need to link my Steam account to find a Helldivers squad?
faq.a: No. You can join the lobby and post LFG with any Weered account (Steam, Xbox, PSN, or email). Linking Steam adds presence (other members can see you're in-game).

guides.tldr: Open the Path of Exile lobby on Weered, pick the room matched to your goal (league-launch crew, mapping party, boss service, currency trade coordination, lab/heist runs). Posts include the current league name, level range, party type, and trade-league/hardcore status. GGG OAuth linking shows your verified character + ascendancy.

native_lfg.name: In-game /global 820 (party-finding channel) + Discord servers
native_lfg.honest_take: PoE's in-game party-finding channel works for instant party joins. Discord servers handle persistent league-launch crews and boss-service coordination. Neither has GGG-authenticated character data inline or rich rooms organized by activity. Weered's PoE lobby is built around the league cycle: pre-launch crew formation, day-1 leveling, mapping rotation, endgame chase.

activity_types.name: League launch crews
activity_types.examples: Day-1 leveling party, fresh-economy mapping group
activity_types.typical_filters: League name, target play time, hardcore/SC, voice required

activity_types.name: Mapping parties
activity_types.examples: T16 rotation, atlas completion, juiced maps
activity_types.typical_filters: Map tier, juice level, MF responsibility, atlas completion %

activity_types.name: Boss services
activity_types.examples: Uber boss carries, voidstones, pinnacle bosses
activity_types.typical_filters: Boss tier, payment (free/exalts/divines), proof of completion

activity_types.name: Currency trade coordination
activity_types.examples: Bulk trades, standing exchanges, real-time negotiation
activity_types.typical_filters: Currency pair, volume, hot trade time

activity_types.name: Lab / Heist runs
activity_types.typical_filters: Lab difficulty, heist target, key requirement

guides.weered_advantage: GGG OAuth linking shows your verified active character, ascendancy, and league participation — so boss-service requests come with real proof of completion, and league-launch crews can size up actual progression. Rooms are organized around the league cycle (launch → mapping → endgame → wind-down) so finding the right party doesn't require digging through unrelated chat.

faq.q: How do I find a Path of Exile party for league launch?
faq.a: Open the PoE lobby on Weered before the league starts. The league-launch crew room handles day-1 party formation: post your target play time, hardcore/SC preference, voice requirement, and goal (leveling, mapping rotation, MF crew). Most crews form 2-3 days before launch.

faq.q: Can I find boss-service runners on Weered?
faq.a: Yes. The boss-service room covers Uber bosses, voidstones, and pinnacle fights. Service runners post their pricing (free, exalts, divines), tier eligibility, and recent proof of completion via linked GGG character.

faq.q: Does the Weered PoE lobby link to my GGG account?
faq.a: Yes — optional GGG OAuth linking pulls your active character, ascendancy, league participation, and recent boss completions. Makes LFG posts credible without screenshot proof.

faq.q: Is the PoE lobby on Weered free to use?
faq.a: Yes. Joining the PoE lobby and finding parties is free. Premium tier exists for cosmetic flair but doesn't gate party-finding.

guides.tldr: Open the D&D lobby on Weered, pick the room matched to what you're looking for (player seeking campaign, DM seeking players, one-shot pickup, west marches, specific edition/system). Posts include your role (player or DM), preferred edition (5e, 5.5e, OSR, 3.5e, Pathfinder), schedule, system experience, and tone (combat-focused, RP-heavy, gritty, comedic).

native_lfg.name: r/lfg, Roll20 LFG, D&D Beyond forums, StartPlaying
native_lfg.honest_take: D&D LFG is heavily fragmented: r/lfg for free games, StartPlaying for paid DMs, Roll20's LFG board for platform-specific groups, D&D Beyond forums for 5e-focused matchmaking. Each has its niche. Weered's D&D lobby focuses on persistent groups that stay together past one session — the recurring Tuesday-night campaign rather than the one-shot pickup.

activity_types.name: Long-form campaigns
activity_types.typical_filters: Edition, schedule (day/time), experience level, tone, in-person/online platform

activity_types.name: DMs seeking players
activity_types.examples: DM has a campaign concept, building the table
activity_types.typical_filters: Campaign concept, system, schedule, experience level required

activity_types.name: Players seeking campaign
activity_types.examples: Player available for the right group
activity_types.typical_filters: Edition preference, schedule availability, experience level, character concept

activity_types.name: One-shots and pickup games
activity_types.typical_filters: Module, system, schedule (one date)

activity_types.name: West marches / drop-in
activity_types.typical_filters: World setting, session schedule, character creation rules

activity_types.name: Alternative systems
activity_types.examples: Pathfinder, Call of Cthulhu, OSR, Blades in the Dark
activity_types.typical_filters: System, schedule, experience

guides.weered_advantage: Persistent rooms designed around tabletop's recurring-schedule rhythm (most D&D LFG tools optimize for one-shot pickups). The voice rooms support full session playback, and the forum layer handles campaign session-zero discussions, character backstories, and between-session play.

faq.q: How do I find a D&D group online on Weered?
faq.a: Open the D&D lobby and pick the room matched to what you want: 'Players seeking campaign,' 'DMs seeking players,' 'One-shots and pickups,' or system-specific rooms (Pathfinder, OSR, etc.). Post your preferred edition, schedule, tone preference, and experience level.

faq.q: Can I find a DM on Weered or only fellow players?
faq.a: Both. The lobby has dedicated rooms for DMs seeking players (DM posts the campaign concept and recruits a table) and players seeking campaigns (player posts availability and character preferences).

faq.q: Does Weered support systems other than D&D 5e?
faq.a: Yes. The lobby has rooms or post tags for Pathfinder, OSR, Call of Cthulhu, Blades in the Dark, and other systems. 5e is the largest by traffic but the lobby is system-agnostic.

faq.q: Is the D&D lobby on Weered free?
faq.a: Yes. Joining the lobby, posting LFG, using voice rooms for sessions, and the forum layer are all free. Premium tier exists for cosmetic flair but doesn't gate any tabletop functionality.
